key_insight: A certified reachable-set over-approximation for nonlinear reaction--diffusion PDEs can be built by chaining FEM discretization, POD reduction, and set-based reachability on the ROM with explicit uniform error bounds.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a tractable way to verify safety and uncertainty envelopes for PDE-governed systems such as diffusion-reaction processes without solving the full infinite-dimensional problem directly.
method: Discretize the PDE with FEM to obtain a finite-dimensional surrogate.
Apply POD to derive a reduced-order model with quantified reduction error.
Perform set-based reachability on the ROM and propagate FEM/ROM error bounds back to the original PDE.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an illustrative validation on the Allen--Cahn equation and a logistic growth PDE, with bounds proven uniformly over the parameter set.
watch_item: The certification depends on the quality of the FEM and POD approximations, so coarse meshes or insufficient modes can weaken the reachable-set enclosure.
paper_title: Certified Reachable Sets for Nonlinear Reaction--Diffusion Systems
